p_1_3.disclosed: min over {2..64} of T(n,1/3) = 28/27 at n = 3

anomalies: A1: disclosed practical-grid min at p=2/5 is a drafter slip: the registration claims 'min over {2..8} of T(n,2/5) = 419/375 at n = 3', but the TRUE min over {2..8} is T(8, 2/5) = 3463137/3125000 ~ 1.10820384 at n = 8 (the value 419/375 ~ 1.11733 is T(3,2/5), a LOCAL min, not the grid min). Cause pinned: above the wall T(n,2/5) = 1/n + 1 - (3/5)^n descends toward 1+ as n grows (1/n dominates the vanishing (3/5)^n), so the pool-grid minimum sits at the LARGEST n, not at the wall-optimal n=3. The R1 damage clause STILL FIRES on the true min (3463137/3125000 >= 21/20, margin x1.05543222857 vs the disclosed x1.0641), so the ruling is untouched.

A2: disclosed grid min at p=1/3 is the same-family slip: the registration (F3) claims 'min over {2..64} of T(n,1/3) = 28/27 at n = 3', but the TRUE min over {2..64} is T(64, 1/3) = 223189448317832719882042779499841/219755764498720799018102341713984 ~ 1.01562499999 at n = 64 (28/27 ~ 1.03704 is T(3,1/3), the small-n LOCAL min; the tail dips below it from n=27 on and bottoms at n=64). Same mechanism as the p=2/5 slip: above the wall the pool-grid min sits at the largest n. This anchor is pure reporting (the 'double knife-edge' note); no decision clause reads it, so the ruling is untouched. The n=3 VALUE 28/27 and its status as a local min are both correct.

A3: disclosed 'best batch above the wall wastes 11.7%' inherits the same slip: the best PRACTICAL batch at p=2/5 is n=8 wasting 10.82% (T=3463137/3125000), not 11.7% (which is n=3's waste); direction and order of magnitude stand.

arm_r.note: REPORTING-ONLY; no statistical gate rides Arm R

bands.approve: min{2..64} T(n,2/5) <= 1 AND pair <= triple somewhere

falsifiability_world.note: delete the 1/n pool-test cost and the wall vanishes; the verdict flips to APPROVE outright

margin_ledger.R1_strict_note: theorem: T(n,2/5) > 1 for every n in {2..64}; infimum over unbounded n is 1 (grid-bounded by design; n=64 tail = 1.015625)
margin_ledger.T3_zero_note: q^2 = 1/2 is the family's one true margin-0 cell; irrational, off every rational grid point (certified symbolically)

wall.certificate: pooling helps at p for some n in {2..64} iff 3q^3 > 1
